segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with two men walking outdoors in a sunny, arid environment with palm trees and stone walls, as upbeat, driving electronic music begins. One man, in a black shirt, states their goal to "train like a pro triathlete for six days." The scene quickly shifts to a cyclist in a green jersey and white helmet riding through a desert landscape, exclaiming excitedly, "I went above 90!" accompanied by a whoosh sound. This is followed by a swimmer in a wetsuit and swim cap entering a pool, as a coach aggressively yells, "Stop fucking around now, get swimming!" The electronic music continues with a vocal sample. The narrative then cuts to a man in a white and green jersey, visibly dehydrated, standing in a rocky desert, stating, "And I'm so dehydrated." This is followed by a close-up of a cyclist in a red jersey and white helmet, frustrated and holding his head, groaning and saying, "I don't wanna train." Another man, in a green jersey and black helmet, complains about "working the fucking wind." The electronic music continues as a voice declares, "Welcome to the real world," and another adds, "The wind is fucked up," before the music fades slightly. The scene returns to the two men walking outdoors, now with the man in the black shirt speaking animatedly to the camera, gesturing with his hands, introducing the video from Lanzarote, which he describes as "the Mecca for triathletes worldwide," explaining they are there for a training camp before an Ironman, as advised by "beloved Uncle Freek." He continues, detailing their journey "all the way from Brussels to Lanzarote to train like a pro triathlete for six days." Upbeat Latin-infused music with Spanish singing then begins, and a voice welcomes viewers to a "new video." The setting changes to an airport terminal on May 14th, 2023, where the two men, now in casual travel attire, are pulling suitcases. The man in the black shirt announces it's "14th of May, 6 o'clock in the morning," as they head to "drop off our bikes." The final shot shows them boarding an Iberia airplane, with one man smiling at the camera as they settle into their seats, accompanied by the continuing Latin music.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the Man in airport (white shirt) and the Man in airport (black shirt) exiting an Iberia airplane at Madrid airport. Upbeat, energetic music plays as the Man in airport (white shirt) excitedly exclaims, "Just arrived in Madrid! Oh my God! Oh my God! It's my bike! Oh my God!" upon spotting his bicycle on the tarmac. The scene transitions to the Iberia airplane's interior with passengers boarding, then a surreal composite shot of a plane flying low over a beach. The upbeat music continues as the Man in airport (black shirt) mentions "Gate number two" and "Happy hour, no?". The two men are then seen arriving in Lanzarote, walking across the tarmac under a bright, windy sky, with a male voice noting, "Very windy, Lanzarote." The music transitions to a more relaxed, acoustic guitar tune as they enter a modest apartment. Inside, the Man in airport (white shirt) is shirtless, unpacking, and revealing a side tattoo. He gestures around, giving a friendly "house tour," stating, "Hi everybody, welcome to this house tour. So, this is where we sleep. I sleep here, Arno sleeps here," and pointing out "Arno's closet here." He then opens the door to greet the Man in airport (black shirt), who enters wearing a black Nike shirt and sunglasses. The Man in airport (white shirt) exclaims "Oh, Arno!" with a laugh, and the Man in airport (black shirt) responds with laughter. The Man in airport (white shirt) asks "Why are you so surprised?" before showing "This is my closet." The Man in airport (black shirt) points to a photo on the wall as the Man in airport (white shirt) describes "This is our living room," and then introduces "our god, the one and only, Nocafé." The Man in airport (black shirt) then moves toward the kitchen area, where a blue bicycle is visible. The segment concludes on a sunny rooftop terrace, with the Man in airport (black shirt) smiling and gesturing. The Man in airport (white shirt) explains, "Voilà, so we already unpacked a little. So this is Arno's bike, my bike," as the sound of wind is heard, indicating they are preparing for their next activity.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens on a sunny rooftop terrace where the Man in black shirt, wearing a black athletic shirt with sunglasses perched on his head, animatedly reads from a piece of paper, his voice clear and determined, detailing their training plans. The Man in blue shirt, in a blue Garmin-branded shirt, listens intently, occasionally gesturing or reacting with surprise. Sounds of paper rustling accompany the Man in black shirt's speech as he reads about becoming an 'Iron Man' and a training schedule from 'Fre,' including an inspirational quote: 'that triathlon is not going to train for itself. So we go on a training camp.' He then outlines their first day's plan: an 8-kilometer run, followed by a recovery shake, water, dinner, and early bed, emphasizing that tomorrow will be 'double training.' After a distinct sound of a high-five or clap, the scene cuts to a first-person perspective of the Man in black shirt running on a gravel path in a dry, rocky desert landscape under a bright blue sky. Upbeat electronic music begins as on-screen text announces 'DAY 1 TRAINING 1 8KM - RUNNING'. The Man in black shirt, now running, speaks to the camera with determination, confirming it's their 'first training day in Lanzarote.'

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the Man in black shirt, wearing a black athletic shirt and blue reflective sunglasses, running along a gravel path in a dry, rocky desert landscape under a clear blue sky. He speaks directly to the camera in a Belgian/European accent, explaining he's running on little sleep after a DJ set and an early flight, but feels good. The scene shifts to the Man in blue shirt, wearing a blue Garmin-branded athletic shirt, black cap, and white earbuds, also running through the desert with the sun low on the horizon. He shares that this is the 'first kilometer in' of 'Hell Week' training, feeling heavy from lack of sleep but looking forward to the week. The two men briefly interact; the Man in black shirt asks, 'Are you sure this is the way?', to which the Man in blue shirt replies, 'I think so, yeah.' The Man in black shirt then comments, 'It looks like the middle of a desert,' prompting the Man in blue shirt to respond, 'Okay, we might be a little bit lost.' They continue running, with the Man in black shirt checking in, 'All good, still feeling it?' and receiving an affirmative reply. He then remarks on the beautiful scenery, noting it's 'definitely different than training in rainy Belgium,' and expresses feeling well despite the lack of sleep, excited for the week. As the environment transitions and they approach a paved road with parked cars and traffic signs, the Man in blue shirt comments on 'a cold wind.' The Man in black shirt asks, 'How's it going?', and the Man in blue shirt replies, 'Good,' adding that he was 'really stiff in the beginning' but is 'loosening up' in this 'weird terrain' of the desert. The Man in black shirt runs across a crosswalk, smiling and gesturing to the camera, while the Man in blue shirt follows, still focused on his phone, as the setting becomes more urban.

The Man in black shirt adds, "Easiest training of the whole week... prepare for what's coming tomorrow," and they share playful banter. The video ends with a view of the apartment bedroom, showing a messy bed and a blue curtain, before cutting to a title card reading 'DAY 2', accompanied by calm, reflective background music featuring piano and strings.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a man in a white t-shirt with 'average' printed on it, smiling under a large white umbrella, as he enthusiastically declares, "Beautiful day, let's go!" The scene quickly transitions to a bright, sunny beach near calm water, bordered by a stone wall and palm trees, where the same man, with a clear and enthusiastic voice, interacts with another man in a black t-shirt and green patterned shorts. They discuss swimming in the lagoon, with the man in the black shirt mentioning, "We're swimming in the lagoon today," and the man in white adding, "Not hot, but uh... average!" followed by laughter and applause. The man in white then explains they'll be coached for open water swimming by a professional triathlete and world record holder. As a third man, wearing a black shirt, purple shorts, and a pink cap, walks toward them, a social media overlay identifies him as 'Sam Laidlow,' an athlete and Kona bike course record holder, with the caption 'LET'S GET THIS BOOTY TO THE TOP.' The man in white introduces him, saying, "Please make some noise for Sam Laidlow!" to cheers and applause. Sam, with a distinct voice and slight accent, greets them warmly with a hug, joking, "Try to make you guys suffer!" The trio stands together by the water, continuing their conversation. The man in white refers to Sam as "My triathlete," and the man in black shirt shares his experience, stating, "I actually have talent for swimming, I think. I survived the water." Sam vividly describes the challenges of open water swimming, noting how it "feels like forever" and how one might think they're halfway only to find they've swum "400 meters." He also reveals his pre-swim ritual: "Normally I get on my knees before I swim and you baptize yourself."

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens inside a modern apartment where the Man in white and green jersey and the Man in black shirt are preparing for a cycling session. The Man in white and green jersey, organizing energy bars and a water bottle on a table, discusses needing "two gels an hour" for "seven hours today," totaling "14 gels." As upbeat electronic music begins, the Man in white and green jersey, now wearing a black helmet, mounts his blue road bike with 'DT SWISS' wheels. The Man in white and green jersey, now with blue-tinted sunglasses, explains that "yesterday was such a tough training" and they "didn't know the route," introducing a new rider named "Laurence." The Man in black shirt adds that Laurence is "a really good triathlete from Belgium." The Cyclist in green jersey confirms, "Today we're gonna do the Yaiza loop," which includes a "National Park" and "vineyards." The Man in white and green jersey details the elevation challenges: "Times three, 2,100 altitude meters," while the Man in black shirt adds that in Klagenfurt, they'll have to do "between 1,400, 1,500." The Cyclist in green jersey concludes it will be "the perfect training today." After a "Good luck, boys" from the Man in white and green jersey and a "Bye" from the Man in black shirt, the scene shifts to an outdoor setting with a paved road and volcanic terrain. Inside a corridor with terracotta tiles, the Man in white and green jersey removes his helmet and sunglasses, revealing curly hair, and prepares his white road bike, mentioning he needs "to take some more gels." segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a white road bike's front wheel and handlebars, showing a 'CONTINENTAL' tire and a black stem, as the rider navigates a paved road beside a rocky, volcanic terrain. A male voice, belonging to the rider, comments on nearly falling, saying, "like uh, two mullets on the ground. Look at this. Ay, ay, ay. But I'm fine. Well, I was lucky. I was very lucky. Imagine falling at this speed. Almost fell off the bike two times, in the downhill, and the wind just wanted to give me a butt hit, but I'll keep going. I have to literally stop to eat because there's too much wind." The camera then shifts to a first-person perspective of the Man in white and green jersey, wearing a white Canyon helmet and blue-tinted sunglasses, speaking to the camera while riding through a scenic, arid landscape under a partly cloudy sky, with background music playing. He asks, "Hello, hello. How's it going? Lap two went well. You don't look tired at all. No, I'm really good. The gelikas are really good. What the hell? So now we need to refuel one last time and then go full patat again." He transitions to wearing gold-tinted sunglasses, continuing to speak as he rides past white-walled buildings and palm trees, stating, "I'll be back in exactly two hours. Ciao, ciao. Two more hours, come on." The scene cuts to a third-person view of the same cyclist riding through a parking lot, passing parked cars and a white van, before turning onto a road. He then dismounts and walks his bike toward a modern building labeled 'Club La Santa', where he waves to the camera, while commenting on the intense wind, "There is so much more wind. Yo, I'm 1993, something like that. The wind is just fucked up. I don't even know if you can hear me." Another voice interjects, "Rob fell," which the rider confirms, "Rob fell. Yeah, he fell. segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the Man in white and green jersey, wearing a white Canyon helmet and blue-tinted sunglasses, retrieving a water bottle from a white locker in a modern corridor, accompanied by muffled chatter and a male voice commenting, "It's weird when you listen," followed by laughter and an encouraging "run, run, run, run." Another voice wishes "Good luck," to which the Man in white and green jersey replies "Thank you" after being told "Don't fall," promising "I'll try." He then transitions to riding his white road bike outdoors under a bright blue sky, stating, "50 more kilometers. Last lap. Come on," and describing the "First part is always the hardest. 7 kilometers going up." As he rides through a scenic coastal area and into barren volcanic terrain, he lets out a grunt of exertion, exclaiming "Fuck," and reveals, "The climbing starts and there's something wrong with my derailleur." Loud mechanical clicking, grinding, and clanking sounds are heard, which he points out, saying, "You hear this sound? This is not normal for a bike." The camera cuts to the Man in black shirt, wearing a green cycling jersey and black helmet, pedaling vigorously on his blue road bike, with a Garmin device displaying metrics like heart rate (171) and distance (144.82 km), while the Man in white and green jersey continues to diagnose his bike issue, stating, "Okay, I lost my two lowest [gears], try third," then correcting himself, "Okay, no, I lost my three lowest." He reappears, speaking directly to the camera with a wide, sweaty smile, exclaiming, "Only 20 more kilometers to go and the wind, it's fucking annoying!" He adds, "I'm riding 13.8 in time trial modus, it's just... worst thing about Lanzarote is the fucking wind," concluding with "Look." The scene dynamically shifts from indoor preparation to intense outdoor cycling, emphasizing physical exertion, scenic environment, and the challenges faced.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a first-person perspective of a cyclist riding a white road bike along a straight, empty road through a barren, volcanic landscape under a bright blue sky. The Man in white and green jersey, wearing a white Canyon helmet and blue-tinted sunglasses, comments with an accented voice, "Almost flat. Going so fucking slow." Cinematic music begins as the scene cuts to a low-angle shot of the rocky, gravelly terrain. A close-up then shows the Man speaking directly to the camera with an energetic expression, reflecting, "That was the most horrible beautiful thing I've ever done in my life. The wind, third loop was 10 times worse than the second," adding, "I know he's gonna cry." He continues riding his white road bike through a parking lot and a corridor with white walls and palm trees, as a Strava activity overlay appears, detailing 'IRONMAN TRAINING DAY 119'—a 165.05 km ride with 2,117 m elevation gain, noted as his longest. He announces, "Straight into 4K run," and exclaims, "Fuck, longest day of my life!" He then plans, "I think I need to run 5:30 the kilometer. 40 more minutes." The music continues as he dismounts, the sound of bike gears audible, and walks his bike toward a blue running track. Removing his helmet, he grunts, "Oh my god," and transitions into a 4km run, as indicated by a text overlay. He expresses disbelief, "Can't believe it. For lowest start from here." The camera captures him running on the track, with palm trees and white buildings in the background, his heavy breathing and running sounds audible. He exclaims in French, "Aiyayay. Ça fait mal," indicating pain. A low-angle shot shows him adjusting his bike, now wearing a green cycling jersey, before he resumes running. The final frames show him running on the track, a Strava overlay confirming a 4.20 km run at a pace of 5:17/km, concluding with a smile and a thumbs-up to the camera.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens in a sunny parking lot where Marco, wearing a white Castelli jersey, blue-tinted sunglasses, and a heart rate monitor, greets Arno, who approaches on a blue road bike with 'DT SWISS' wheels, wearing a green cycling jersey, black helmet, and pink socks. Arno adjusts his bike and checks his Garmin device, recounting, "was a problem, I fixed it. I went angry," to which Marco replies, "You went uh..." Arno continues, "Third lap I didn't..." punctuated by a distinct bike gear shifting sound, then exclaims, "I didn't realize... Holy shit balls, Marco," revealing Marco's name. Arno adds, "Bro, I went Super Saiyan on the last lap." The scene transitions to a bright rooftop terrace with white buildings and palm trees, where the two men continue their conversation, accompanied by Spanish-style background music. Marco comments, "Little hacker," then asks, "Oh, you don't have your watch?" Arno replies, "No, I put it on the table. In the apartment." Marco confirms, "Yeah," then adds, "Oh, then I don't have it." Marco states, "I told him," to which Arno playfully responds, "Yeah, I'm not your slave, eh," and then asks, "Who forgets his watch?" The scene shifts to Arno running on a bright blue synthetic track, surrounded by green grass, palm trees, and white buildings, as the Spanish background music intensifies with a male vocalist singing. A Strava overlay appears, displaying his "Avondloop" activity: 4.09 km distance, 6:21/km pace, and a time of 26m 2s, identifying him as "arno The Kid." Marco comments, "Nice shot," as the music continues. After finishing, Arno walks back to Marco, who is now sitting on a low wall. Arno remarks, "I'm actually not that tired. I thought I was going to be..." before Marco interjects, "So proud of us." They share a high-five and a laugh. Arno then humorously adds, "I have to take a shit, bro. What the fuck?" to which Marco replies, "Really proud." Arno removes his jersey, revealing his chest and a black heart rate monitor, as Marco praises him, "You did it. It was amazing. You did it too." They sit together, drinking from water bottles, with Marco gesturing animatedly, while Arno reflects, "160k, 4k run. We did it and still feeling good actually." Arno outlines his immediate plans: "Protein shake, little shower, 'cause I need it. And tomorrow we do it again." The Spanish music fades out, replaced by a gentle, melancholic guitar melody, as the sunny, coastal training environment is clearly visible.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a bright 'DAY 4' title card against a backdrop of white buildings, a vibrant green plant, and a clear blue sky, accompanied by upbeat acoustic guitar music. Average Rob, wearing a black swim cap with red camouflage patterns, goggles, and red swim trunks, is seen completing a 3,400m swim session in a modern outdoor pool with blue and white lane dividers and festive bunting. His voice, with a distinct Belgian/Dutch accent, details his plan: "So today we need to do 3K of swimming and then 80K bike riding," adding "It's going nice, I gotta go. Bye bye." He continues swimming, using a black swim paddle, explaining his remaining distance and technique: "300 meters left, just with the bare hands and I have 800 with pedals and then cooling down." He notes the 50-meter pool "feels a bit like open water because it's also super deep," and outlines the final sets: "4 times 200 and then 200 cooling down and then we're done. Let's go!" After finishing, he rests at the pool's edge, removing his cap and goggles, checking his smartwatch, and speaking to the camera, sounding relaxed and satisfied: "Alright, we're done. 3.4K. Feeling nice, feeling good. It's such a nice place here." On-screen text confirms his Strava activity as 'IRONMAN TRAINING DAY 120'. He then plans the next steps, stating, "Then we're gonna get some lunch and then we're gonna go for a bike ride." The scene transitions to an outdoor dining area at 'EL LAGO' under large white umbrellas, where Rob, now in a maroon t-shirt, sits with a companion in a black shirt, enjoying a colorful meal and red juice. The companion, a man in a black shirt, exclaims "Oh, wow!" and "Man, wow!" and later says "Gracias." Rob's voiceover explains they are "Loading up the carbs," but expresses frustration that "Our planning today was is a bit shit. We still have 80K to ride. It's 3:30."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens at El Lago, where Average Rob, sporting a mustache and a maroon t-shirt, engages with the camera while eating a colorful dish under large white umbrellas. Across from him, the Man in black shirt, in a black t-shirt, focuses on his meal, using a fork and knife, with a blue water bottle and phone beside him. The Man in black shirt's voice immediately sets a humorous tone, explaining their cycling dilemma: they need to wait an hour for food to digest, then it will be 5 PM before they leave, followed by a 3 to 3.5-hour ride, making it too late for food. He suggests faking an injury 'with Frea' or doing a shorter 60km ride. The scene transitions to an indoor apartment. The Man in black shirt, now shirtless in black underwear, gestures in a bathroom, his voice continuing to express reluctance, stating, 'I don't wanna train,' and proposing a 55km loop instead of 80km, as 80km would take three and a half hours in Lanzarote. Average Rob, now in a red and white cycling jersey, white helmet, and red-tinted sunglasses, walks through the apartment, adjusting his gear, pausing to look at a photo taped to the wall, and filling a water bottle in the kitchen. Average Rob's voice interjects enthusiastically, 'So I can break my record,' and 'My 92.56 kilometers an hour!' The Man in black shirt responds, 'No, don't do it,' and later, 'He's gonna be extremely disappointed. Frea for today,' followed by laughter. The Man in black shirt then appears in a black cycling jersey and helmet, adjusting gear near a blue 'DT SWISS' bicycle. His voice continues, 'Don't look. I'm not a big fan of training. I've been complaining the whole day.' Average Rob checks his Garmin device. segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with close-up shots of two cyclists, Average Rob and the Man in black shirt, both wearing helmets and sunglasses, speaking to the camera against a backdrop of a bright blue sky with scattered clouds and a volcanic landscape. It's all bumps, so it's like... Turbo, turbo, turbo." He emphasizes the strong, audible wind, stating, "As you can hear, there's a lot of wind." The camera then captures Arno riding down the steep road at high speed, with the camera following from behind and then from a side angle, showcasing the dramatic descent through the rugged landscape. An upbeat, driving instrumental track begins as he descends. The road is flanked by dark volcanic rock and sparse vegetation, with distant hills and a few scattered buildings visible under the partly cloudy sky. On-screen text overlays provide context, including phrases like 'Arno's 92 km/h descent' and 'It's all bumps, so it's like... Turbo, turbo, turbo,' highlighting the intensity of the ride. The segment concludes with Arno continuing his descent, the wind whipping around him and the sounds of his bike on the bumpy road, as he navigates the steep, winding road.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a first-person perspective of a hand pointing toward a desolate volcanic landscape beside a paved road under a bright blue sky, followed by a view from a moving vehicle showing cacti and distant hills. Upbeat, rhythmic string music begins as Average Rob, in a red jersey, cycles his white road bike along a winding road through the barren terrain. He stops at a roundabout, checking his phone, which displays a Strava activity overlay for 'IRONMAN TRAINING DAY 118' showing 55.19 km. Average Rob greets "Hello" to Arno The Kid, who joins him, also checking his Strava data. Average Rob's voice is heard stating "55 kilometers" and humorously adding, "If it's not on Strava, it never happened." The two cyclists interact, sharing water, with Average Rob offering, "You want Arno?" to which Arno replies, "I didn't drink enough." Average Rob, gesturing and smiling, discusses his energy, remarking, "I'm not tired, it's crazy. My legs were feeling super good. Once the motor was warm, I was feeling like super good. I really like this ride. Might be one of my favorite ones," while Arno questions, "Really?" Average Rob then declares, "Day four is a wrap. Now we need to get some food again, reload some proteins, to get these muscles," making playful grunting sounds and adding, "Look at these jambons." The string music returns as the scene shifts to a bright, sunny day with an on-screen title card reading 'DAY 5 last training day' and 'IRONMAN SIMULATION 3000 m'. Average Rob's voice continues, setting the stage for "day five, final day also of our training camp here. We're going to do a simulation today, an Ironman simulation," as a man in a white t-shirt with 'Stop Working Start Drinking' on the back walks along a palm-lined path with a blue bicycle toward a coastal area with white buildings and a pool.

The scene shifts to a concrete seating area under a covered structure labeled 'Club La Santa', where they sit and prepare. The Man in white t-shirt (curly hair) removes his shirt, revealing a tattoo, while the Man with mustache unpacks a pink and green triathlon suit from a bag, explaining it's "full weird" and "based off of one of my favorite movies of all time." The Man in white t-shirt (curly hair) interjects, "I think I know what it is," before excitedly exclaiming, "It's a Borat suit!" as the Man with mustache laughs and confirms, "On a triathlon suit." Upbeat, slightly comedic music begins as the Man with mustache puts on the suit, zipping it up, and stands up, smiling, ready for the simulation. segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with upbeat Latin/Reggaeton music featuring a female vocalist as the Man in white t-shirt (mustache) swims freestyle in a bright, modern outdoor pool, accompanied by splashing and muffled underwater sounds. After an on-screen text '1 HOUR LATER', he emerges, smiling in a pink and green triathlon suit, pulling himself up a metal ladder. His Strava overlay reveals 'IRONMAN TRAINING DAY 121' with a 3,050m swim completed in 50m 59s. He encourages himself with a clear, articulate voice, saying, "Yeah, come on bro, come on. Alrighty. Let's go. Time to waste," before wrapping himself in an orange towel. He explains, "Gonna dry up because I don't want to get cold. There's a lot of wind," as he walks briskly to a covered area. Preparing for the next leg, he puts on a white cycling helmet and red-tinted sunglasses, then mounts a blue 'DT SWISS' bicycle. As he rides through a dry, rocky desert landscape under a clear blue sky, a close-up shows him speaking, with 'TRIATHLON PT. 2 40KM - BIKE RIDE' on screen. He reflects on his swim, stating, "Alright, just had to switch. Went quite slow, I'd say, but I'd prefer it to be slow and good and to be in a hurry and forget shit. Swimming went well. In the beginning, my arms were a bit heavy, but once they were warmed up, it actually went quite well, so happy with the swim." The scene briefly cuts back to the pool where Arno The Kid exits the water, his Strava showing a 2,950m 'Lunch Swim'. Arno remarks in a distinct voice, "Done. It's not amazing," as he sits on a concrete bench, drying off with an orange towel. The Man in white t-shirt continues his ride in the desert, and a brief exchange is heard where Arno asks, "How was your run?" and the Man in white t-shirt replies, "Good. And you're not too far behind," as the upbeat music continues, emphasizing the transition from the structured poolside to the open, arid terrain.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the Man in white t-shirt (mustache), wearing a pink and green triathlon suit, sitting on a concrete bench at Club La Santa, adjusting his gear. He reflects in a male voice that someone was "really good" and "helped my technique," making him feel like "a kid next to him." A bell rings, followed by farewells like "Bye-bye," "Good luck," and "Ciao" from multiple voices. He then puts on a black cycling helmet, stands, mounts his blue bicycle, and begins riding along a paved road through a desert landscape. The sound of wind picks up as he states, "Just riding through the desert." The camera switches to a first-person perspective, showing the road, sky, and his helmet. He exclaims "Oh shit," and "Oh my god," noting "there's like these sand spots, it's so sketchy, holy shit," indicating a challenging terrain. He then reflects on his performance, mentioning the "first 10k" where he "went really fast" and "a lot of aero," even with "a lot of wind." With the wind still audible, he declares, "20 more to go, let's go," and then, "Let's go, a little climb now." He expresses satisfaction, stating "all these months of training are paying off," and that his "legs feel really good," allowing him to "go deep and then recover really fast." The wind continues to blow as he says, "Let's go!" He stops to eat a banana, holding it up to the camera before taking a bite, explaining that "the fourth discipline of a triathlon is eating," and it's his "favorite thing to do in the world." He emphatically calls out "Carbs! Carbs!" before concluding, "One more kilometer and we're there. Can't wait to see Milan. He's gonna be so proud, I can feel it." The bright, sunny desert environment with sand dunes, sparse vegetation, and distant mountains remains visible throughout his determined ride.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with the Man in a pink and green triathlon suit, identified as Arno, riding a white road bike through a dry, rocky volcanic landscape under a partly cloudy sky, accompanied by upbeat electronic music. A voice exclaims, "And 16K run, let's go!" As Arno passes parked cars and approaches the 'Club La Santa' building, he comments, "The extra mash, I like," and humorously adds, "My name is Borat. The extra mash." He continues, "I like this, I just did 40 kilometers bicycle ride," before stating, "Let's go for the switch up," as bicycle chain shifting sounds are heard. He dismounts, removes his helmet, and enters the modern building. Inside, he changes into orange running shoes, preparing for the next leg. Another voice asks, "How was Arno's swim? Was he happy?" to which Arno, breathing heavily, replies, "Yeah, he was happy, he was fresh," and later, "Feeling fresh." The voice remarks, "You look full triathlete now," and Arno confidently responds, "Yeah, now I look like a pro." After more bicycle chain sounds, he exits the building, jogs through the parking lot, and transitions to running on a gravel path through the desert. The scene briefly shows him riding a blue bicycle through a roundabout. Back at the building, Arno sits, checking his Garmin device which displays metrics like 144.82 km distance and 43:55 time. He explains his plan: "Four loops of 4 kilometers, 16K, and then we're done for today." The other voice echoes, "The extra mash," and Arno enthusiastically declares, "I like running, you like running? Okay, very nice!" The voice urges, "Holy shit, let's go, let's go!" Arno confirms, "I'm good, I'm good," and when asked "How's your run?" he replies, "Good." After a final "Jesus" and "Ciao, ciao. Good luck," Arno mentions "Three kilometers" before a "Bye bye." The video concludes with a title card reading 'TRIATHLON PT. 3 16KM - RUNNING' against the volcanic terrain, as the electronic music continues. Strava overlays throughout the segment show details like 'IRONMAN TRAINING DAY 121' and 'Afternoon Ride' for 'Arno The Kid'.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of the Man in white t-shirt (mustache), wearing a pink and green triathlon suit, orange-tinted sunglasses, and a black cap, running along a paved path. He speaks directly to the camera with a clear, slightly accented voice, expressing satisfaction about finishing the first loop, feeling good and not exhausted, and determined to maintain his pace. The environment is bright and sunny, with a clear blue sky, scattered clouds, and a dry, rocky landscape featuring sparse vegetation and palm trees. He gestures animatedly, at one point raising his fist in excitement, and later turns to face the camera with a wide smile. As he runs, the camera occasionally shifts to show the road and surrounding terrain, including a white wall, a parking lot, and distant hills. He exclaims "Look at that badass!" followed by laughter, as the Man in white t-shirt (curly hair) appears, running alongside him. The Man in white t-shirt (curly hair), with a distinct voice, asks "How are you feeling?", to which the Man in white t-shirt (mustache) replies "Good, and you?", and the Man in white t-shirt (curly hair) responds "Amazing!", reinforcing their energetic interaction with a high-five and a moment of celebration. The Man in white t-shirt (mustache) continues, explaining that the beginning was "a bit stiff" from the bike and swimming, causing sore arms, and recalls a 3km run from yesterday. He then mentions seeing "Arno" looking "super fresh," expressing pride and confidence in him for the 16k. He humorously admits being "scared to fart" due to potential "wet fart," followed by laughter, then states "the rest, amazing." He notes "good tempo, good heartbeat," and again humorously mentions needing to fart but refraining. He states he still needs to complete "one loop" and expresses extreme dehydration, asking "Can I get a bit of water?" to which the Man in white t-shirt (curly hair) readily agrees, "Yeah, sure." Sounds of gasping, gulping, and water pouring are heard as he drinks, commenting "It's warm, eh?" with a sigh of relief. The Man in white t-shirt (curly hair) then asks "Do you have water?", and the Man in white t-shirt (mustache) confirms, offering it: "Yeah, yeah, yeah. Take it." More sounds of rustling, water pouring, and gulping suggest continued hydration. He continues running, occasionally glancing at his watch, his determined expression and wind blowing through his curly hair captured against a coastal view with the ocean, a roundabout, and a parking area with white buildings. The overall atmosphere is energetic and focused, capturing the intensity and joy of a long-distance run in a scenic, arid environment.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Arno, the Man in white t-shirt (mustache), running along a gravel path in a dry, rocky desert landscape under a clear blue sky, wearing his pink and green triathlon suit. He remarks, "It's so weird. I'm not kaput," as he runs, later confirming, "Hey, it is my last lap, eh?" and receiving encouragement like, "You're doing great." He continues running, expressing exertion with "Yes, it's coming," before slowing to a walk on a paved area near Club La Santa. He acknowledges the distance, "16," and his fatigue, "Ah, well done, man. Oh, the legs," then declares, "Voilà, that's done. Easy." An on-screen Strava overlay appears, showing 'IRONMAN TRAINING DAY 121' with 16.00 km and a 5:31/km pace, noting he set his 3rd fastest time in the 15k. He adjusts his red-tinted sunglasses and is greeted by the Man in white t-shirt (curly hair), also in a triathlon suit, who comments, "Light work," and encourages him, "Let's go, man. Come on. 200 more. Come on, Arno!" They share a high-five in the parking lot, where another Strava overlay for 'Arno The Kid' shows 16.04 km and a 6:43/km pace. The two men stand together, catching their breath, removing their sunglasses, and conversing casually in front of the Club La Santa building. Their conversation includes humorous remarks about needing the toilet ("Have to shit," "You have to shit, yeah?") and a post-run debrief where Arno states, "My muscles... muscles are done," and his companion adds, "Muscles, yeah, for me too." They celebrate their accomplishment with exclamations like, "We fucking did it! We did it, motherfucker!" and discuss their performance, concluding with "Let's get a shower." The atmosphere is bright and sunny, filled with a sense of accomplishment and camaraderie.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of the Man in white t-shirt (mustache), now wearing a pink and green triathlon suit, engaged in conversation with the Man in white t-shirt (curly hair) in a similar suit on a rooftop terrace under a bright blue sky. The Man with the mustache, speaking in a deeper voice, discusses taking a "cold shower" and enjoying the "chill pool for a moment," to which the Man with curly hair responds with an "Ohhh." The scene transitions to a wide shot of the two men walking along a paved path beside a large swimming pool, with white buildings and palm trees in the background under a golden-hued sky, suggesting late afternoon or early evening. They sit down on a concrete ledge near the pool, the Man with curly hair holding a Corona Extra and the Man with the mustache holding a dark beer. They clink bottles, and the Man with the mustache toasts, saying "Santé, to a very productive week. I'm very proud of us." He then adds, "I'm proud of you," to which the Man with curly hair replies, "And I'm proud of you." The Man with the mustache then addresses him by name, "I'm proud of you, Milan," and Milan thanks him.
